steps.title: Identify the key requirement
steps.content: The conclusion's central claim is that distinguishing spontaneous from volitional laughter is universal across cultures. To strengthen this, the new finding must reveal a consistent, objective feature that exists everywhere—something all listeners could reliably detect.

steps.title: Match to the strongest evidence
steps.content:
Choice B identifies a measurable acoustic feature (higher pitch in spontaneous laughter) that holds true across all societies, providing an objective, consistent basis that listeners everywhere could use to distinguish laugh types. The other choices either address unrelated abilities, suggest variation that contradicts the universality claim, or vaguely describe acoustic differences without establishing consistency.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
